text: **Anna:** {Penelope|人名|/pəˈnɑːləpi/}, even though we both love {speedwalking|速歩き|/ˈspiːdwɔːkɪŋ/|Speedwalking is a competitive sport.}, can we slow down a bit?
**Penelope:** No way.
**Penelope:** I think because I'm walking {faster|より速く}, I'm walking faster.
**Anna:** Come on, Penelope.
**Anna:** Let's take a {break|休憩}.
**Anna:** We've been speed walking since 8 a.
**Penelope:** m.
**Anna:** You're so {competitive|競争心の強い}.
**Anna:** You even wore your {medals|メダル}.
**Penelope:** You brought your {trophies|トロフィー}?
**Penelope:** You're out of {shape|体型/調子}.
**Penelope:** When we were little girls, we could speedwalk all day.
**Anna:** Hey, wait for me!
**Anna:** You know the speedwalking {rules|ルール}!
**Anna:** If both feet leave the ground, it's running!
**Penelope:** You ran!
**Anna:** Anna and Penelope are doing something they both love.
**Anna:** Speedwalking.
**Anna:** They are also using {adverb|副詞の} clauses.
**Anna:** Adverb clauses tell us when, why, how, where, and under what conditions.
**Penelope:** For example, when we were little girls, tells us when.
**Anna:** Adverb clauses are {dependent|依存する} clauses and need an {independent|独立した} clause to make a complete sentence.
**Anna:** Words like before, after, when, because, since, if, and even though can signal an adverb clause.
**Anna:** Keep watching for more adverb clauses.
**Anna:** Woo!
**Penelope:** That was great.
**Anna:** Wow Penelope, you're still really fast since I've been working in an {office|事務所}.
**Anna:** My speed walking has been much slower.
**Penelope:** You are slower, Anna.
**Penelope:** Maybe you should hire a {personal trainer|パーソナルトレーナー}.
**Anna:** That will be {great|素晴らしい/素晴らしいこと|/ɡreɪt/|It's a great day today.}
**Anna:** As long as the {trainer|トレーナー/指導者|/ˈtreɪnər/|My trainer helps me improve.}
**Anna:** {Understand|理解する/わかる|/ˌʌndərˈstænd/|Do you understand the rules?} speed walking.
**Anna:** Try this {company|会社/企業|/ˈkʌmpəni/|This company is very successful.}
**Anna:** After you fill out an {online|オンラインの|/ˈɒnlaɪn/|I do online shopping.} {application|申請書/応募用紙|/ˌæplɪˈkeɪʃn/|Fill out the job application.}, they will match you to the perfect trainer.
**Anna:** As soon as I get home, I'll do it.
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Anna:** Hi, are you my {trainer|トレーナー/指導者|/ˈtreɪnər/|My fitness trainer is strict.}?
**Alaskan Albert:** If you're Anna, then I'm your trainer.
**Alaskan Albert:** Alaskan Albear.
**Alaskan Albert:** Because I'm from the {Alaskan|アラスカの|/æˈlæskən/|Alaskan winters are cold.} {wilderness|荒野/自然|/ˈwɪldərnəs/|The wilderness is beautiful.}
**Anna:** I'm skilled in all {outdoor|屋外の/外の|/ˈaʊtdɔːr/|Outdoor activities are fun.} {activities|活動/行事|/ækˈtɪvətiz/|We have many school activities.} of the wild
**Anna:** That's great, but what about speed walking?
**Anna:** {Speedwalking|スピードウォーキング|/ˈspiːdwɔːkɪŋ/|Speedwalking is a sport.}?
**Alaskan Albert:** That's funny.

**Alaskan Albert:** Since I've been living in the city, I've {combined|組み合わせた/結合した|/kəmˈbaɪnd/|Combined efforts are powerful.} {wilderness|荒野/自然|/ˈwɪldərnəs/|The wilderness is vast.} training with a city workout.
**Alaskan Albert:** I call it Call of the Wild Parkour.
**Anna:** Okay
**Anna:** But seriously, when do we {speedwalk|速歩する|/ˈspiːdwɔːk/|Athletes speedwalk for exercise.}?
**Anna:** Your {application|申請書/応募用紙|/ˌæplɪˈkeɪʃn/|Fill out the application form.} did not say how funny you are.
**Anna:** No, but it did say that I'm a {speedwalker|速歩競技者|/ˈspiːdwɔːkər/|A speedwalker trains hard.}
**Anna:** Did you read it?
**Anna:** Forget it.
**Anna:** We'll have to {cancel|キャンセルする/取り消す|/ˈkænsəl/|We must cancel the meeting.}
**Alaskan Albert:** Why?
**Anna:** We can't train today because it's too {cold|寒い/冷たい|/koʊld/|It's very cold outside.} and too {windy|風の強い|/ˈwɪndi/|Windy days are challenging.}
**Penelope:** No way.
**Anna:** No pain, no gain.
**Anna:** When you {exercise|運動する/エクササイズする|/ˈeksərsaɪz/|I exercise every morning.} in really cold weather, you feel alive.
**Anna:** Really?
**Anna:** Because right now I just feel {cold|寒い/冷たい|/koʊld/|The weather is cold today.}
**Alaskan Albert:** You're so funny.

**Alaskan Albert:** But seriously, after our {training|訓練/トレーニング|/ˈtreɪnɪŋ/|Athletes need regular training.}, you will be ready for any {extreme|極端な/過激な|/ɪkˈstriːm/|An extreme sport requires skill.} physical challenge.
**Anna:** Let's get started.
**Alaskan Albert:** Anna, even though you're trying really hard, you're really bad at Call of the Wild Park.
**Anna:** I'm bad at Call of the Walk
**Alaskan Albert:** Call of the Wild {Parkour|障害物競走|/pɑːrˈkʊər/|Parkour involves jumping over obstacles.}!
**Anna:** Because I don't want to do Call of the Wild Parkour!
**Anna:** I'm a {speed walker|速歩競技者|/spiːd ˈwɔːkər/|Speed walkers move quickly.}!
**Anna:** It's what I do
**Anna:** It's part of who I am.
**Alaskan Albert:** Why didn't you say something?
**Anna:** I did several times.
**Anna:** You thought I was joking!
**Alaskan Albert:** Now I understand.
**Alaskan Albert:** Anna, I can help you.
**Anna:** If you follow my advice, I'll put the speed back in your speed walk
**Anna:** Go on a go!
**Anna:** For more about {adverb|副詞|/ˈædvɜːrb/|Adverbs describe actions.} clauses, visit our website.
